# DATPAQ Convert Time API Sample (Detailed)

## Purpose
This app demonstrates how to call the DATPAQ Convert Time API from a frontend SPA while showing robust request/response UX states.

## API endpoint
- Base URL: `https://datpaq.com/api`
- Route: `GET /v1/convert-time`
- Full reference: `https://datpaq.com/api/v1/convert-time`

## Authentication
The backend requires an API key in the request header:
- Header: `x-api-key: <your_api_key>`

## Request parameters
- `sourceTime` (required): ISO 8601 string, epoch seconds, or epoch milliseconds.
- Source input (choose one):
  - `sourceZone` (IANA timezone)
  - `sourceLocation` (human location)
- Target input (choose one):
  - `targetZone` (IANA timezone)
  - `targetLocation` (human location)

## Expected successful response shape
- `success`: boolean
- `data`:
  - `sourceTime`
  - `targetTime`
  - `sourceTimezone` details
  - `targetTimezone` details
- `meta`:
  - `processingTimeMs`
  - `timestamp`
  - `correlationId`

## Expected error response shape
- `success`: false (or absent depending on service behavior)
- `error` or `message`
- `meta` (optional)

## UX behavior in this sample
- Validates required fields before requesting.
- Uses GET query parameters for all conversions.
- Shows loading, error, and success blocks.
- Displays copyable request URL and response JSON.

## Machine-readable schema
- OpenAPI document is provided at: `/openapi.json`
